2604.12033.title: Benchmarking Deflection and Hallucination in Large Vision-Language Models
2604.12033.paper_summary: Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) increasingly rely on retrieval to answer knowledge-intensive multimodal questions. Existing benchmarks overlook conflicts between visual and textual evidence and the importance of generating deflections (e.g., Sorry, I cannot answer...) when retrieved knowledge is incomplete. These benchmarks also suffer from rapid obsolescence, as growing LVLM training sets allow models to answer many questions without retrieval. We address these gaps with three contributions. First, we propose a dynamic data curation pipeline that preserves benchmark difficulty over time by filtering for genuinely retrieval-dependent samples. Second, we introduce VLM-DeflectionBench, a benchmark of 2,775 samples spanning diverse multimodal retrieval settings, designed to probe model behaviour under conflicting or insufficient evidence. Third, we define a fine-grained evaluation protocol with four scenarios that disentangle parametric memorization from retrieval robustness. Experiments across 20 state-of-the-art LVLMs indicate that models usually fail to deflect in the presence of noisy or misleading evidence. Our results highlight the need to evaluate not only what models know, but how they behave when they do not, and serve as a reusable and extensible benchmark for reliable KB-VQA evaluation. All resources will be publicly available upon publication.
2604.12033.paper_summary_zh: 大型視覺語言模型（LVLMs）越來越依賴檢索來回答知識密集的多模態問題。<lb/>現有的基準忽略了視覺和文本證據之間的衝突，以及在檢索的知識不完整時生成偏離（例如，抱歉，我無法回答……）的重要性。<lb/>這些基準還面臨快速過時的問題，因為不斷增長的 LVLM 訓練集使模型能夠在不進行檢索的情況下回答許多問題。<lb/>我們通過三項貢獻來解決這些空白。<lb/>首先，我們提出了一個動態數據策劃管道，通過篩選真正依賴檢索的樣本來保持基準的難度隨時間變化。<lb/>其次，我們引入了 VLM-DeflectionBench，一個包含 2,775 個樣本的基準，涵蓋多樣的多模態檢索設置，旨在探討模型在衝突或證據不足的情況下的行為。<lb/>第三，我們定義了一個細緻的評估協議，包含四種情境，將參數記憶與檢索穩健性區分開來。<lb/>在 20 個最先進的 LVLM 上的實驗表明，模型通常在存在噪聲或誤導性證據時無法偏離。<lb/>我們的結果突顯了評估模型不僅知道什麼，還要評估它們在不知道時的行為的必要性，並作為可靠的 KB-VQA 評估的可重用和可擴展基準。<lb/>所有資源將在發表後公開提供。
2604.12033.author: Nicholas Moratelli et.al.
2604.12033.authors: Nicholas Moratelli, Christopher Davis, Leonardo F. R. Ribeiro, Bill Byrne, Gonzalo Iglesias
